concerns.text: Task T009 implements the 'future-value estimate' as a 'stub... returning a constant heuristic'. This violates Spec FR-001 and US-1 which mandate the Branching Score be the product of entropy and a 'pre-trained, frozen future-value estimate'. A constant heuristic renders the score non-functional (entropy × constant), effectively removing the core research variable and failing to satisfy the FR.

Task T014 implements a 'No-Score' (standard PPO) training loop. While the spec requires a baseline, the task description lacks the explicit verification step that this baseline runs with the *exact* same hyperparameters (except the score) as the Score-Default variant to ensure a fair comparison, which is a prerequisite for the statistical validity required by FR-007 and US-3.

Task T016 implements 'threshold-not-reached' flagging. The spec (Edge Cases) requires the system to 'record the final performance and steps' in this event. The task description only mentions 'flagging logic' and does not explicitly include the deliverable of recording the final performance metrics, risking a dropped acceptance criterion.

Task T022 specifies running '1 seed per grid combination (12 runs total)'. This contradicts Spec FR-006 which mandates '3 independent training runs (seeds) for each Score-Ablation variant'. Reducing seeds to 1 makes it impossible to perform the Wilcoxon signed-rank tests required by FR-007, violating the testability of the success criteria.

Task T018 specifies executing '3 seeds' for No-Score and Score-Default. This contradicts Spec FR-006 which mandates '5 independent training runs (seeds)' for these configurations. This reduction weakens the statistical power required for the Wilcoxon tests in FR-007 and US-3.
